- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — **`fab memory-index` ships the generated FKF surface** (FKF KEYSTONE, change 2/4). New `### Generated Memory Artifacts (FKF)` subsection records that `fab memory-index` is now the single writer of three artifact kinds, not just `index.md`: (1) `index.md` tiers (unchanged); (2) per-folder `log.md` (C-lite — git history joined with each change's `.status.yaml` `summary:`, date-grouped newest-first, optional bold verb + bundle-relative link + summary/slug + `(change-id)`, empty-folder skip); (3) FKF frontmatter (`fkf_version: "0.1"` on the root index only, plus a `type: memory` preserve-when-present round-trip mechanism — no authoring/bulk-stamp, those are FKF changes 3/4 and 4/4). All three are single-writer/byte-stable and join the `--check` guards as **benign drift (tier 1)** — no new tier-2 category (FKF/OQ4). The commit→change-id join is registry-gated (`fab/changes/*` + `archive/**`) with graceful degradation: against this repo's squash-merged history it recovers ≈0 ids (squash subjects carry no branch token), so most entries fall back to the commit subject with `(change-id)` omitted; self-heals as FKF-era summaries land. Updated the `summary` field bullet (the generator that *reads* it now exists; it never writes the field). Per-file `## Changelog` tables are NOT yet removed (that strip is FKF change 3/4). (bmzo)
- **Update** [clarify](/pipeline/clarify.md) — refactor: SRAD Resolution-Average Confidence Scoring (#420)
- **Update** [clarify](/pipeline/clarify.md) — **Confidence-recompute prose follows the Resolution-Average formula.** The intake score is no longer a grade-count penalty ledger (`base = 5.0 − 0.3·confident − 1.0·tentative`, times coverage) — it is the **mean of the per-row S:R:A:D composites** (`0.25·S + 0.30·R + 0.25·A + 0.20·D`) rescaled `/20` onto 0–5, times the unchanged coverage factor, with per-row Critical-Rule (`R<25 AND A<25`) and any-Unresolved hard-fail short-circuits to 0.0. The § Confidence Recomputation prose was reworded: clarification raises the score because re-grading resolved assumptions raises their per-row composites (lifting the mean), **not** because it "reduces the penalty count" (there is no penalty count anymore). No behavior change in this skill — the score is still recomputed via `fab score --stage intake`, which reads the Assumptions table; only how `fab score` derives the number changed (in `score.go`). The "Grade Reclassification" decision's "scores increase after clarification" remains accurate under the new formula. Formula authority: `_cli-fab.md` § fab score (extended), `docs/specs/srad.md` § Confidence Scoring. (tf5q)

- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Pipeline hydrate stage gains a refuse-before-regen guard** (defense-in-depth site of the glwc destructive-loss guard). Hydrate Behavior Step 5 (Regenerate indexes) now consults `fab memory-index --check` before the `fab memory-index` regen and **refuses on exit 2** (destructive loss — curated description→`—`, tombstone drop, or grouping flatten), surfacing the `→ run /docs-reorg-memory to remediate ...` pointer (the orchestrator that relocates tombstone rows and dispatches `/docs-hydrate-memory` backfill mode for descriptions). The guard is a **no-op for born-compatible fab-kit trees** (a pipeline-hydrated tree is provably never exit 2) — annotated so it is not mistaken for dead code; it is defense-in-depth for the pathological case of a pre-fab-kit tree reaching the pipeline's hydrate stage. The loss logic lives entirely in Go (tiered `--check` exit codes 0/1/2), so the site adds only a one-line exit-code consult — the same primitive `/docs-hydrate-memory` and `/docs-reorg-memory` consult. No other hydrate-behavior change. SPEC mirror: `SPEC-fab-continue.md`. (glwc)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Archive soft-skip now branches on typed resolve errors (Go).** The § Idempotent Re-Archive subsection updated: `archive.go` `Archive` gates the already-archived fallback on `errors.Is(err, resolve.ErrNotFound)` and `batch_archive.go` `runBatchArchive` checks `errors.Is(err, resolve.ErrAmbiguous)` first and surfaces it (warn + continue) — an ambiguous change name is no longer silently soft-skipped as "already archived" (the old string-only path conflated it with not-found). Sentinel/`classifiedError` mechanism (message strings preserved) lives in [schemas.md](/pipeline/schemas.md) § `internal/resolve` Typed Errors. No skill-prose change — this is a binary-behavior correction under documented archive semantics. (jznd)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Two pipeline-choreography prose fixes (skills + SPEC mirrors only — no Go)** (recurring-lessons items 4+5; Group B of 2, merged after Group A `[jznd]`). **Defect (a) — cycle-count invariant**: `_pipeline.md`'s Auto-Rework Loop now states the iteration-counting invariant explicitly — `stage_metrics.review.iterations` is advanced by exactly one event (a review `→ active` transition, `status.go:627`), the `reset apply` cascade preserves-without-incrementing the counter (`status.go:646–660`), so each cycle MUST drive exactly one `finish apply` review re-activation (item 3) and never re-enter review by a non-`active` path. Baseline convention (oracle `TestStageMetrics_IterationsAccumulateAcrossReworkCycles`): iterations == initial entry + each rework re-entry, so initial fail + N cycles → iterations N+1 → `fab pr-meta` "{N+1} cycle(s)" (an initial fail + 2 cycles → "✓ 3 cycles", never "1 cycle"). The under-report was a **prose gap, NOT a Go bug** — the state machine was already correct (no `internal/status`/`internal/prmeta` change; the Go regression oracle already passes). **Defect (b) — review-pr poll discipline**: `git-pr-review.md` Step 2 Phase 2 gained (i) a permanent synchronous-poll / don't-yield-mid-poll directive (the subagent must complete the 30s × 20 poll without yielding — it had stalled/died mid-poll 4×); (ii) the corrected two-login mapping — the landed-review poll predicate matches `author.login == "copilot-pull-request-reviewer"` (the review-author login on the `reviews` array, `[bot]`), distinct from `Copilot` (the login surfacing under `requested_reviewers`); (iii) request confirmation via REST `requested_reviewers` because GraphQL `reviewRequests` omits bot reviewers. Cadence unchanged (30s × 20); the poll stays inside `/git-pr-review` (subagent owns request + poll + triage synchronously — intake Assumption #7). `fab-fff.md` Step 5 dispatch prompt mirrors the don't-yield directive into the dispatch seam. SPEC mirrors updated same-PR: `SPEC-_pipeline.md`, `SPEC-git-pr-review.md`, `SPEC-fab-fff.md` (`fab-ff.md`/`SPEC-fab-ff.md` untouched — no divergent per-cycle wording). The live "PR review handling" prose here was reconciled to carry the corrected login predicate; the historical n30u/u1m1/4ojc changelog rows below already agreed and are unchanged. See [schemas.md](/pipeline/schemas.md) for the state-machine half (cycle-count accuracy is a choreography property, not a state-machine one). (qg64)

- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **Change-type explicit set is now sticky (Go behavior, doc-flagged).** Updated § Change Type (Hook-Owned) and the "Change-Type Inference Is Hook-Owned" design decision (now "…, Explicit Set Is Sticky"): as of jznd `fab status set-change-type` writes `change_type_source: explicit` and the PostToolUse intake-write hook re-infers/overwrites `change_type` ONLY when the source is absent/`inferred`, skipping inference when `explicit`. The long-standing "any subsequent intake edit re-fires the hook and overwrites the override, so re-verify after later intake writes" caveat is **retired** — a human-corrected type survives all later intake writes. Absent/empty source still decodes as `inferred` (back-compat). The g3-4-era rejected option "make the hook respect a skill-set value" was the correct deferral then and was shipped deliberately as jznd. Schema/field/hook-guard detail: [schemas.md](/pipeline/schemas.md) § `.status.yaml` Change-Type Fields. The `fix` regex was also tightened (passing `must-fix` no longer misclassifies a feature intake). (jznd)
- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **Intake confidence score is now a Resolution Average** (Go `score.go` change; SRAD grade-mapping prose unchanged). The gate score replaces the grade-count penalty ledger (`base = 5.0 − 0.3·confident − 1.0·tentative`, times coverage) with the **mean of the per-row composites** — reusing the *same* `0.25·S + 0.30·R + 0.25·A + 0.20·D` weights this doc's SRAD-framework decision documents for grade mapping — rescaled `/20` onto 0–5, times the unchanged coverage factor (`min(1.0, total_decisions / expected_min)` over ALL graded rows). Two per-row hard-fail short-circuits to 0.0 are preserved/added: any Unresolved row, and the per-row Critical Rule (`R<25 AND A<25`) evaluated on raw dimensions before the mean. A 3.0 gate now equals a mean composite of 60 (the Confident floor) — the flat-3.0 gate, the 0–5 scale, and the Assumptions table format are unchanged. `fix` `expected_min` lowered 5→3. Because the score reuses the per-row dimensions, thoroughness with strong dimensions raises it (the old ledger penalized having more rows). No skill behavior changed — `fab score --stage intake` still reads the Assumptions table; only how the number is derived (`score.go`, single `countGrades` parse pass extended with `SumComposite`/`CriticalRowSeen`) changed. Formula prose authority lives in `_cli-fab.md` § fab score (extended) and `docs/specs/srad.md` § Confidence Scoring (both rewritten); `change-types.md`/`glossary.md` aligned. clarify.md's confidence-recompute prose de-penalty-counted (see [clarify.md](/pipeline/clarify.md)). (tf5q)

- **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — **Binary truth-telling (Go).** Added the `## .status.yaml Change-Type Fields` section: new `change_type_source: inferred|explicit` enum (`omitempty`, sparse-insert, `SourceInferred`/`SourceExplicit` constants, absent==inferred back-compat); `set-change-type` marks `explicit`; the PostToolUse intake-write hook re-infers/overwrites `change_type` ONLY when source is absent/`inferred` and skips inference when `explicit` (acceptance counting still runs) — the pre-jznd silent-revert race is gone. `fix` regex tightened (a passing `must-fix` no longer misclassifies a feature intake; `bug-fix`/`hot-fix`/`bug-free`/standalone still classify `fix`; old pattern reused as `fixCandidateRegex` inside `fixSignal`, not deleted). **Acceptance is read-time-derived**: `status.LiveAcceptance(changeDir) (done, total, ok)` counts `## Acceptance` checkboxes in `plan.md` on read; preflight/prmeta/`status plan` prefer it over the persisted `plan.acceptance_*` counter (now a write-time cache/fallback when `ok=false`) — survives sed/direct edits. - **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — **Cycle-count accuracy is a choreography property, not a state-machine one** (skills + SPEC prose only — no Go; the schema half is a clarifying note). Reinforced the k4ge "Cascade preserves `iterations`" bullet: the iterations-preserving `reset`/`skip` cascade is correct **as-is** and MUST NOT be touched. `iterations` advances on exactly one event — a review `→ active` transition (`status.go:627`) — and `reset` neither increments nor zeroes it (`status.go:646–660`), so the truthfulness of `fab pr-meta`'s cycle count depends on the orchestrator skills driving exactly one counted review re-activation per rework cycle. The PR #402 "1 cycle" under-report was therefore fixed in `_pipeline.md`'s Auto-Rework Loop prose (the cycle-count invariant + baseline convention iterations == initial entry + each re-entry, N cycles → N+1), **not** in `internal/status`/`internal/prmeta`. The Go regression oracle (`TestStageMetrics_IterationsAccumulateAcrossReworkCycles`) already passes for the documented sequence — this change touched no Go. See [execution-skills.md](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) § Shared Pipeline Bracket for the choreography half + the review-pr poll-discipline fix shipped in the same change. (qg64)
- **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — **`confidence.score` derivation changed — Resolution Average** (no `.status.yaml` schema change; the block's fields/shape are identical). `score.go`'s `computeScore` now derives the `confidence.score` field from the **mean of the per-row S:R:A:D composites** (`0.25·S + 0.30·R + 0.25·A + 0.20·D`) rescaled `/20` onto 0–5 times the unchanged coverage factor, replacing the grade-count penalty ledger (`base = 5.0 − 0.3·confident − 1.0·tentative`). Per-row Critical-Rule (`R<25 AND A<25`, on raw dimensions) and any-Unresolved rows still hard-fail to 0.0; the per-row composite sum + Critical-Rule flag are accumulated inside the single `countGrades` parse pass (extended `GradeCount` with `SumComposite`/`CriticalRowSeen`), so the public `fab score` CLI signature is unchanged. `expectedMin["fix"]` lowered 5→3. The flat-3.0 gate threshold, the `--check-gate` non-zero exit contract (k4ge), and the normal-mode hard-fail surfacing (hv7t) are all unchanged. Formula prose authority: `_cli-fab.md` § fab score (extended); `docs/specs/srad.md` § Confidence Scoring. The `change-types.md` `expected_min` doc table (the `TestDocTablesMatchScoringMaps` mirror) was updated `fix` 5→3 in lockstep. (tf5q)

The `/fab-proceed` dispatch table's two **create-new rows** now read `_intake → /fab-switch → /git-branch → /fab-fff` (was `/fab-new → /fab-fff`): the create-an-intake sub-operation dispatches the shared `_intake` Create-Intake Procedure (`{questioning-mode} = promptless-defer`) instead of the full `/fab-new` skill, and because `_intake` stops at intake `ready` without activating or branching, the `/fab-switch` (activate) + `/git-branch` (branch) chain is now **required** for parity — **inverting** the w7dp claim that the create-new rows could drop `/git-branch` as a no-op (that held only while they invoked full `/fab-new`, whose Step 11 branched inline). `/fab-proceed`'s state-detection, relevance assessment, asymmetric-bias rule, bypass notes, Conversation Context Synthesis, and terminal `/fab-fff` delegation are all unchanged — only the create-an-intake leaf is rerouted. `/fab-draft` reconciled to a thin `_intake(interactive)` call-site (superseding the szxd "thin delta over fab-new" description; momentum warning retired). - **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Collapsed the post-intake dual execution mode — one execution mode, orchestrators as pure sequencers** (skills+specs prose refactor + one Go test; no production Go change, no new CLI command). Guiding principle (newly named): **intake is the sole context boundary** — the main session runs up to and including intake; after intake the intake artifact IS the context and every post-intake stage (apply/review/hydrate) runs as a dispatched sub-agent fed by `intake.md` + `plan.md` + `docs/memory/`. `fab-continue.md`: the three caller-aware "When invoked as a subagent: do NOT run `fab status`" blockquote conditionals (Apply/Review/Hydrate Behavior) are **removed** — with one execution mode there is no second branch; plain `/fab-continue` is now a **one-stage sequencer** that runs `fab resolve-agent <stage>` then dispatches the stage's block exactly as the orchestrators do, and owns the `finish`/`fail`/`reset` transition after it returns. The "do NOT run `fab status`; return results only" prompt instruction is now the **universal block contract** (carried in the dispatch prompt), not a per-caller skip rule in the block; it is NOT re-encoded as any "skip §Verdict when subagent" flag. The interactive § Verdict rework menu (Path A) and `_pipeline.md`'s autonomous Auto-Rework Loop (Paths B/C/D) **remain** as the invocation-level failure-policy fork. Header note reconciled from "foreground = advisory-only" to the one-stage-sequencer framing. `_pipeline.md` Behavior note affirmed as **pure sequencer** (dispatch → read → proceed/loop/stop) with the universal-contract framing; per-cycle rework choreography (fail+reset pair) and the history-shape invariant **unchanged in semantics**. `_preamble.md` § Per-Stage Model Resolution "Foreground is advisory-only" minimally reconciled (residual advisory only for a genuinely no-dispatch run; closes Gap 1a — uniform tiering across apply/review/hydrate; full uniform-tier closure deferred to a follow-up). New Go test `TestHistoryShape_IdenticalRegardlessOfDriver` in `internal/status/mutators_test.go` pins that the manual (`driver=""`) and dispatched (`driver="fab-fff"`) paths leave `.history.jsonl` entries of identical shape — agreeing on every caller-blind field (stage/action/from/reason), equal modulo the per-run `ts` timestamp and the optional `driver` field (see [schemas.md](/pipeline/schemas.md)). SPEC mirrors updated same-PR: `SPEC-fab-continue.md` (f006 → universal block contract), `SPEC-_pipeline.md`, `SPEC-_preamble.md`, `docs/specs/stage-models.md` § Foreground limitation. `runtime/operator.md` verified — no foreground-post-intake assumption (no-op). (fgxx)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Per-stage dispatch sites surface the resolved tier + inject effort via the subagent prompt (two-seam dispatch contract)** (Change C — skills/docs prose only, no Go; gated on `260613-fgxx`). Every per-stage dispatch site in the `fab-ff`/`fab-fff` orchestrator family — the shared `_pipeline.md` bracket (Behavior "Per-stage model resolution" note + Steps 1 apply / 2 review / 3 hydrate + Auto-Rework re-apply/re-review items 3/4) and `fab-fff.md` Steps 4–5 (ship/review-pr) — now (1) **surfaces** the resolved `model=/effort=` after `fab resolve-agent <stage>` (carried into the dispatch prompt and/or echoed in step output, so a skipped/mis-resolved tier is **visible rather than silent** — Gap 1b compliance visibility), and (2) applies the tier through **two seams**: the **model** half via the Agent tool's `model` param (unchanged) and the **effort** half via an imperative instruction in the subagent prompt (``Operate at `<effort>` reasoning effort for this task.``, omitted when empty — Gap 2's effort half, previously left as fgxx's deferred follow-up; the Agent tool has no `effort` param). `review` resolves **once** for both reviewers + merge (same model + same effort instruction). `fab-continue.md`'s one-stage-sequencer header note + post-intake dispatch contract + nested-reviewers note carry the same two-seam phrasing; `fab-ff.md`'s sibling note (structural twin of fab-fff's) reconciled to match. The harness-adapter boundary records the **id→alias** detail (the `model` param takes `opus`/`sonnet`/`haiku`/`fable`, not the full `claude-*` id the resolver emits). The lone residual is the **harness ask** — a first-class per-sub-agent `effort` param on the Agent tool, out of fab's control, not built. SPEC mirrors updated same-PR: `SPEC-_pipeline.md`, `SPEC-_preamble.md`, `SPEC-fab-fff.md`, `SPEC-fab-ff.md`, `SPEC-fab-continue.md`. `docs/findings/per-stage-model-tier-application.md` (Gap 1a closed by fgxx, Gap 1b + Gap 2-effort addressed here, item 4 = residual) and `docs/specs/stage-models.md` reconciled. Canonical contract: [_shared/context-loading.md](/_shared/context-loading.md) § Per-Stage Model Resolution. (m3d4)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Every Agent-tool-dispatch `fab resolve-agent` call repointed to `--alias`** — the deterministic Go-side id→alias adapter that supersedes m3d4's prompt-side hand-map. The orchestrator/sequencer's per-stage model half is now resolved with `fab resolve-agent <stage> --alias` (the flag emits the Agent-tool-valid short alias directly on the `model=` line) at every dispatch site: `_pipeline.md`'s per-stage-model Behavior note + Steps 1/2/3 (apply/review/hydrate) + Auto-Rework re-apply/re-review items 3/4, `fab-fff.md` Steps 4–5 (ship/review-pr) + its per-stage-model note, `fab-ff.md`'s sibling note, and `fab-continue.md`'s one-stage-sequencer header note + post-intake dispatch contract + nested-reviewers note + dispatch-table rows (apply/review/hydrate). The Status-transition-ownership prose here (the pure-sequencer paragraph) is repointed: the model half resolves with `--alias`, no agent hand-maps the id. The effort seam (imperative subagent-prompt instruction) is unchanged from m3d4. The operator path deliberately stays on the plain `fab resolve-agent apply` (CLI `--model` accepts full IDs) — see [runtime/operator.md](/runtime/operator.md). Go: new `--alias` flag on `resolveAgentCmd` + exported `agent.ModelAlias` (prefix-matched, empty/non-Claude verbatim). The m3d4 row below is preserved as historical record of #413. Canonical contract + Design Decision: [_shared/context-loading.md](/_shared/context-loading.md) § Per-Stage Model Resolution + § `--alias` Flag. (yky7)

- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **Pre-boundary intake-creation extracted to `_intake`** (Change B of the skill-architecture refactor trio): `/fab-new` Steps 0–9 lifted into the internal helper `src/kit/skills/_intake.md` (flat canonical source; deployed `.claude/skills/_intake/SKILL.md`), parameterized by one knob `{questioning-mode}` (`interactive` | `promptless-defer`) applied only at Step 8. The three consumers became thin call-sites: `/fab-new` = `_intake(interactive)` + its Steps 10–11 activate/branch **tail**; `/fab-draft` = `_intake(interactive)` stop-at-`ready` (its szxd "thin delta over fab-new" form and the "don't activate/branch by momentum" warning **retired** — the not-to-run steps no longer live in any body draft executes); `/fab-proceed` dispatches `_intake(promptless-defer)` and its create-new rows now chain `_intake → /fab-switch → /git-branch` (the w7dp "drop `/git-branch` — fab-new Step 11 branches inline" claim **inverted**: `_intake` stops at `ready` without branching, so the chain is REQUIRED for parity). `fab-new`/`fab-draft` declare `helpers: [_generation, _srad, _intake]`; `_intake` carries no `helpers:` (references `_generation` Step 5, `_srad` Steps 4/8 in-body); `/fab-proceed` keeps no `helpers:` (dispatches the helper to a subagent). Step 4 genericized ("the invoking skill" not "this `/fab-new` invocation"); Step 5 delegates to `_generation.md` § Intake Generation Procedure. `_preamble.md` allowlist 6→7 values (+`_intake`). New § The `_intake` Shared Create-Intake Procedure + § Promptless Dispatch rewrite; new design decision "Pre-Boundary Intake-Creation Extracted to `_intake`". The `/fab-proceed.md` heading renamed `§ fab-new Dispatch` → `§ Create-Intake Dispatch`; line-46 cross-ref reconciled in lockstep. SPEC mirrors: new `SPEC-_intake.md` + `SPEC-fab-new`/`SPEC-fab-draft`/`SPEC-fab-proceed`/`SPEC-_preamble` updated. Pure skill restructure — zero Go code; behavioral parity is the bar. (3xaj)

- **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — **History shape is caller-identity-blind** (test-only Go addition; no production state-machine change). New `TestHistoryShape_IdenticalRegardlessOfDriver` in `internal/status/mutators_test.go` pins that the rework choreography `Finish(intake) → Finish(apply) → [Fail(review) → Reset(apply) → Finish(apply)]×N` leaves `.history.jsonl` transition entries that agree on every caller-blind field (count/stage/action/from/reason) — equal modulo the per-run `ts` timestamp and the optional `driver` annotation — whether driven with `driver=""` (manual/foreground) or `driver="fab-fff"` (dispatched) — because `driver` flows only into `applyMetricsSideEffect`, never into a state transition. New "History shape is caller-identity-blind" bullet under § Transitions documents the invariant. This is the structural reason the post-intake dual-execution-mode collapse (fgxx — see [execution-skills.md](/pipeline/execution-skills.md)) was safe: both modes already issue the same CLI call sequence. (fgxx)

- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — **`stage_hooks` documented** (skills-audit batch 4/5, Theme 5 — remove-dead/document-live disposition; no Go changes, `config.go`/`status.go` untouched): the live-but-previously-undocumented `stage_hooks.{stage}.pre/post` config surface now has a canonical reference in `_cli-fab.md` § stage_hooks, summarized here under Status Tracking — pre hooks block `fab status start` on non-zero exit (transition not applied); post hooks run after `finish`'s transition is saved; `finish`'s auto-activation does NOT fire the next stage's pre hook; **failing-post-hook re-run trap** (stage already `done`, re-running `finish` errors on done→done — run the hook by hand or `reset` first). Execution: `sh -c` from the repo root, stdout/stderr passthrough, absent = silent no-op; the scaffold does not seed the key, and migration `2.1.6-to-2.2.0.md` explicitly preserves it. - **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — **`DisplayStage` gained a `failed` tier** (`src/go/fab/internal/status/status.go`): the display walk is now active → failed → ready → last done/skipped → first pending, returning the FIRST failed stage in pipeline order as (stage, `failed`). `active` stays first (in-progress work supersedes a parked failure); `failed` outranks `ready`/`done` so a parked failure is no longer masked by the last-done fallback — a review-failed change with nothing active now displays `review — failed` in `/fab-status`, `fab change list`, and preflight `display_stage`/`display_state` instead of `apply — done`, aligning display with `/fab-continue`'s existing review-failed dispatch row. `CurrentStage()` routing deliberately untouched. State vocabulary `failed` row corrected to `review, review-pr` (matches `AllowedStates`). Motivated by exposing the state half as `fab pane map --json`'s new `display_state` field (see [pane-commands.md](/runtime/pane-commands.md)) — the backlog's "failed/ready = needs human" unlock requires `failed` to be reachable. (dkn3)
- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — **Branch-management hardening** (skills-audit batch 3/5, behavior-flagged). `/git-branch` Step 2 now splits resolution failure by stderr: ambiguous **multi-match** (`Multiple changes match`) STOPs with the candidate list — no junk standalone branch from a false "No matching change found"; true no-match keeps the standalone fallback (doc-only — stderr phrases verified in the binary, no Go change). Both branch twins (git-branch Step 4 / fab-new Step 11) gained: a **remote-only** case — `git checkout --track "origin/{branch}"` instead of recreating a divergent local; the rename guard extended to rename when the current local-only branch resolves to the **same** change being branched (worktree-placeholder fix); and the non-blocking **dirty-tree carried-over note** on create/rename actions (warn, never stash-prompt; fab-new's `{dirty_count}` excludes `fab/changes/{name}/` — the twins' single deliberate divergence). Git Integration table updated to 7 rows accordingly. New **default-branch resolution convention** documented: symbolic-ref → `gh repo view` → literal fallback, used by `/git-pr` (guard + nudge) and `/fab-operator` (fetch-first cherry-pick/rebase base), inlined per consumer. `SPEC-git-branch.md`/`SPEC-fab-new.md` mirrors updated same-PR. (g8st)
- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — Backlog scan honesty (binary-review batch B2/6). `backlog.ParsePending` → `([]Item, error)`: open/read failures surface instead of returning a silent nil, and `fab batch new --list`/`--all` propagate them non-zero rather than rendering a shrunken list. Parsing moved to the whole-file `internal/lines` helper — a >64KB backlog line can no longer truncate the scan (items below it vanished from `--list`/`--all`, and real IDs after it errored with a misleading `not found`). `ExtractContent` errors made accurate (`not found in backlog` only for genuinely missing IDs; real error otherwise); per-ID warn-and-skip in `fab batch new` preserved, now printing the actual error. New "Backlog scan honesty" paragraph after the k4ge archive-exit-semantics paragraph. Sibling hv7t updates: `fab score` failure surfacing in [schemas.md](/pipeline/schemas.md), archive/restore index atomicity in [execution-skills.md](/pipeline/execution-skills.md), `internal/lines`/`internal/atomicfile` packages in [kit-architecture.md](/distribution/kit-architecture.md). (hv7t)
- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — CLI exit-code contract conformance (skills-audit batch 1/5). **Switch Next-line off-by-one fixed**: `fab change switch` now prints the routing stage with the command that drives it (`intake`/`apply`/`review`/`hydrate` → `/fab-continue`, `ship` → `/git-pr`, `review-pr` → `/git-pr-review`), and bare `Next: /fab-archive` only when every stage is `done`/`skipped` — previously it printed `NextStage(routing)` with a shifted command map, showing `/fab-archive` while review-pr work remained. New "Next-line derivation" paragraph under `/fab-switch`. **Archive exit semantics documented**: `fab change archive` zero-args is a usage error (`ExactArgs(1)`); genuinely-archived re-archive soft-skips (exit 0, `already archived:` notice); partial failure (move ok, backlog mark failed) is YAML + non-zero; `fab batch archive` empty `--all` set exits 0 with zero footer, named archived targets count skipped, all-named-invalid keeps exit 1. **`stage_metrics` cascade**: entries with `iterations > 0` now survive the reset/skip cascade (timing fields cleared, counter kept) so `fab pr-meta` cycle counts are real. Also corrected pre-existing drift: the all-done routing fallback returns `review-pr` (not `hydrate`), and tier 2 keys on the last `done`/`skipped` stage. (k4ge)
- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — **Shared-state concurrency + durability for `.status.yaml` and `.fab-status.yaml`** (binary-review B1, F02/F03/F05–F08). New `internal/lockfile` flock helper (sibling `.lock`, bounded ~10s `LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB` retry) serializes all `.status.yaml` load-mutate-save cycles: the 12 mutating `fab status` subcommands (`withStatusLock`), the artifact-write hook, and `score.Compute` — read-only commands stay lock-free; lock siblings gitignored in the repo AND the scaffold fragment (`.status.yaml.lock`). Hook bookkeeping collapsed to ONE Save per event via non-saving `Apply*` variants + `score.ComputeWithStatus` (one folder resolution, one status load, one intake read — was 4 Saves/3 resolutions/2 loads/2 reads); external contracts unchanged. `statusfile.Save` gained fsync-before-rename (source of truth; runtime file dropped fsync — durability follows criticality). Pointer hardening: `setActivePointer` (temp symlink + `os.Rename`) gives `switch`/`rename` an atomic swap (no empty-pointer window, no EEXIST race; failed rename-update now leaves the old pointer, not none); `resolveFromCurrent` stats the pointer target and falls through on dangling pointers without removing the link (was: silent stale-folder answer); `Archive` captures the active pointer by direct readlink BEFORE the folder rename (prerequisite — `pointer: cleared` still reported for the active change). Error truth-telling: `statusfile.Load` wraps non-IsNotExist read failures with their cause; `fab change list` warnings echo the actual load error. Sparse-file writes persist via generalized `syncToRaw` key insertion; `SetProgress` creates missing stage keys and errors on malformed `progress:`, propagated through all six transitions (the `stage_metrics`-persists-while-transition-drops inconsistency is gone). `_cli-fab.md` error-table rows extended to the dangling-pointer case. Updated here: Access pattern, symlink lifecycle, Resolution pattern step 1, new Write concurrency & durability block, Listing Changes warning, archive pointer-capture note, 3 new + 1 updated Design Decisions. (mz4q)
- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — **`review: failed` is now a deliberate resting state** (skills-review batch 4/4, f019/f071): the `/fab-ff`/`/fab-fff` auto-rework loop's per-cycle choreography is stated once in `_pipeline.md` — the fail+reset pair fires on every failed review verdict that starts a new cycle, and exhaustion after 3 cycles fires `fail` only (no reset), leaving `review: failed` with apply `done` as the defined terminal state. Updated "Review failure backward movement" and "Pipeline shortcuts": a user-invoked `/fab-continue` on the failed state presents the rework menu directly (its new review-failed dispatch row replaces the failed→`start review` resume guard); orchestrator re-runs keep the `start review` recovery in `_pipeline.md` Resumability. The failed→active `start` transition row and the state vocabulary are unchanged — no Go/state-machine change. (szxd)
- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — **Git Integration reconciled to the state-bookkeeping vs ship-path split** (skills-audit batch 2/5): fab's state stays git-decoupled during development (no `branch:` field), but `/git-pr`/`/git-pr-review` now enforce branch↔change correspondence with a **hard guard** — STOP before any status mutation/commit/push when the current branch doesn't match the resolved change (two-form match: exact folder equality or folder-as-substring; recovery via `/git-branch`//`/fab-switch`/explicit `<change>`; no autonomous checkout; detached HEAD routes to each skill's detached-HEAD STOP). The former non-blocking git-pr Step 1b mismatch nudge is **removed** — the default-branch-resolution paragraph no longer cites it. `/fab-switch`'s `{state}` qualifier now enumerates all six display states `DisplayStage` can emit (`active`/`failed`/`ready`/`done`/`skipped`/`pending` — verified against `status.go`). `/fab-status`'s progress legend gained **`⏭ skipped`**, matching the Go `ProgressLine` renderer. SPEC mirrors (SPEC-fab-switch, SPEC-fab-status, SPEC-git-pr, SPEC-git-pr-review) + `docs/specs/architecture.md` § Git Integration updated same-PR; the explicit `<change>` argument itself is documented in [execution-skills.md](/pipeline/execution-skills.md). (w7dp)

- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Git-state hardening for the autonomous skills** (skills-audit batch 3/5, Theme 3 — every item a flagged behavior change). `/git-pr`: detached-HEAD STOP ahead of the Step 2 guard (empty `git branch --show-current`; previously committed then emitted a refspec-less `git push -u origin`), Step 1b nudge skipped on empty branch; Step 1 resolves `{default_branch}` (symbolic-ref → `gh repo view` → probed literal `main`/`master`, always non-empty) for the Step 2 guard + nudge; Step 3a replaces `git add -A` with the **expected-area guard** — evaluated before anything is staged (STOP listing untracked files outside `source_paths`/`docs/`/`fab/`; absent config → `docs/`+`fab/`), then `git add -u` + in-area untracked; Step 3 branches on the previously-unread PR `state` — OPEN short-circuit / CLOSED fresh PR / MERGED STOP at Step 3 entry (Key Properties idempotency row now conditions "already shipped" on an OPEN PR). `/git-pr-review`: Step 5 failure handling **split** — commit-fail = reset+STOP (no partial state, true there); push-fail = **keep the commit** + recovery guidance + STOP without replies (the false unsplit "(no partial state)" claim stranded fixes permanently); new **unpushed-commit re-run gate** before "No changes needed" (`@{u}..HEAD`, no-upstream = unpushed → push, then replies cite the now-pushed SHA); Step 6 exception text matches the split. `/fab-archive`: "safe to re-run" qualified with the **dirty-tree disclosure** (uncommitted moves + backlog/index edits for the caller to commit; no autonomous commit — `/git-pr` owns commits); Go `ArchiveWithBacklog` still attempts `backlog.MarkDone` on `ErrAlreadyArchived` (best-effort, error propagation unchanged — exit-code semantics stay k4ge's seam) + unit test + `_cli-fab.md` row. New design decision "Autonomous Git Paths Verify State Before Mutating". Twin-table/branch edits documented in [planning-skills.md](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) + [change-lifecycle.md](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md); operator default-branch resolution in [runtime/operator.md](/runtime/operator.md). Six SPEC mirrors updated same-PR. (g8st)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — Archive/restore index integrity (binary-review batch B2/6). All three archive-index writers (`updateIndex`, `backfillIndex`, `removeFromIndex`) now write atomically via the new `internal/atomicfile` helper and read whole-file via `internal/lines` — a truncated scan can no longer silently delete index entries below a >64KB line on restore, and an interrupted write leaves old-or-new content, never a torn intermediate. **Honest reporting on both paths**: `updateIndex` → `(status, content, error)` (content passed to `backfillIndex`, no post-write re-read) and `removeFromIndex` → `(string, error)`; an index failure yields `index: failed` in the Archive AND Restore YAML, the result is returned alongside the error (the `ArchiveWithBacklog` partial-success pattern — backlog mark still attempted, errors joined), and the CLI prints the YAML then exits non-zero (restore handler now mirrors the archive handler's print-then-error). `fab batch archive` counts a moved-but-index-failed change as archived with a stderr warning, not failed. Missing index on restore stays the benign `index: not_found`/nil. Archive Mode step 2 and Restore Mode step 2 updated; companion `fab score` failure surfacing in [schemas.md](/pipeline/schemas.md), backlog-scan honesty in [change-lifecycle.md](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md), package docs in [kit-architecture.md](/distribution/kit-architecture.md). Docs updated same-PR: `_cli-fab.md`, `fab-archive.md` + SPEC mirror. (hv7t)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — CLI exit-code contract conformance (skills-audit batch 1/5) — archive-seam items. **Re-archive soft skip now real**: `Archive()` gains an archive-scan fallback (`resolveArchive`) so a genuinely archived change (source folder gone) returns `ErrAlreadyArchived` → `already archived: {change}`, exit 0; new "Idempotent Re-Archive (Soft Skip)" subsection under Archive Mode. Bogus names still error non-zero; ambiguous archive matches do not soft-skip; batch archive routes archived named targets through the same path via exported `archive.IsArchived` (counted `skipped`). **Restore `--switch` failure surfaced**: `Restore()` reports `pointer: failed` when post-restore activation errors (skill renders `Pointer: ✗ activation failed — run /fab-switch {name} manually`); `pointer: skipped` strictly means `--switch` not requested. Companion Go/doc fixes documented in [schemas.md](/pipeline/schemas.md) (transition target-state validation, `fab score --check-gate` non-zero gate-fail exit, iterations-preserving cascade) and [change-lifecycle.md](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) (switch Next-line off-by-one, archive/batch-archive exit semantics); `fab hook sync` now honors the "all hook subcommands exit 0" contract (errors to stderr). Skill-doc updates: `_preamble.md` canonical form corrected to `fab resolve --folder`, `_cli-fab.md` rows for all changed behaviors, `fab-archive.md` `pointer: failed` row, `fab-switch.md` Next-line wording + SPEC mirrors. (k4ge)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Skills-review batch 4/4 — twins + self-duplication single-sourcing.** New `_pipeline.md` internal partial holds the shared ff/fff bracket (intake gate, context loading, resumability, Steps 1–3, auto-rework loop, exhaustion stop, shared error rows), parameterized by `{driver}`/`{terminal}`; `fab-ff.md`/`fab-fff.md` shrink to thin wrappers declaring `helpers: [_generation, _review, _srad, _pipeline]` (gate terminology unified to the single-intake-gate framing; post-bail `/fab-clarify` guidance shared so fff regained it). **Choreography formalized (f071)**: per rework cycle the fail+reset status pair repeats on every failed verdict, apply is re-dispatched via an Apply Behavior subagent, and a FRESH review subagent runs per re-review; exhaustion after 3 cycles fires `fail` only (no reset) — terminal state `review: failed`. **fab-continue review-failed dispatch row (f019)**: replaces the failed→`start review` resume guard — `/fab-continue` now resets apply and presents the rework menu directly (no re-review); orchestrator re-runs keep the `start review` recovery in `_pipeline.md` Resumability. `_review.md`'s rework-loop pointer corrected to `fab-continue.md` Verdict + `_pipeline.md` § Auto-Rework Loop. **Self-duplication consolidations**: `fab-archive.md` merged to a single document (restore demoted to `## Restore Mode`; pre-flight waiver preserved as mode-specific); `git-pr.md` unified Step 0 resolves `{name}`/`{has_fab}`/`{has_intake}`/`{change_type}` once (Steps 0a–4c consume the variables; Step 0b/3c names kept for `_cli-fab`/prmeta cross-refs); `git-pr-review.md` states the triage taxonomy once (merged Step 4 classify-and-assign list; Disposition Reference = single reply-format source; Rules cut to 4 non-restated lines incl. the restored idempotency umbrella). New design decision "ff/fff Bracket Single-Sourced in `_pipeline.md`; Exhaustion Terminal State = review failed". SPEC mirrors updated for every touched skill + new `SPEC-_pipeline.md`; `docs/specs/skills.md` § Skill Helpers reflects the 6-value allowlist. (szxd)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **Orchestrator dispatch targets + review-pr failure recovery** (skills-audit batch 2/5, Themes 2+4 — markdown only, no Go changes). `/git-pr` + `/git-pr-review`: optional explicit `<change>` argument (Step 0 transient resolution; **hard STOP** on explicit-arg resolution failure; value-based classification vs git-pr's 7 type tokens) and a **branch-matches-change guard** before any status mutation/commit/push (two-form match — exact folder equality or folder-as-substring; STOP with `/git-branch`//`/fab-switch`/pass-the-change guidance, no autonomous checkout); git-pr's detached-HEAD STOP moved before Step 0a's start (verify-before-mutate parity); the Step 1b mismatch nudge **removed** as superseded. `fab-fff.md` Steps 4–5 dispatch `/git-pr {name}` / `/git-pr-review {name}` (folder name — collision-proof vs type tokens). git-pr-review's Step 6/6.5/phase gates key on "a change resolved in Step 0 (active or explicit)"; push-failure + Phase-2 timeout re-run guidance conditionally name the explicit change. `fab-continue.md`: new **`review-pr`/`failed` dispatch row** (re-execute `/git-pr-review {name}` — `start` owns failed→active; never `reset`, whose From-set excludes `failed`; a failed PR review no longer reads "complete"); ship/review-pr rows pass `{name}` explicitly and cite `active` only (`ready` unreachable per AllowedStates); **idempotent Reset Flow** (target already `active` → skip the CLI call; `failed` → the failed dispatch rows; `pending` → error with guidance); reset event line `done/ready/skipped → active`; `intake.md`-missing error points at `/fab-continue intake`. `_pipeline.md`: the five unexecutable `/fab-clarify intake` recovery pointers replaced by the executable, **override-aware** `/fab-continue <change> intake` → `/fab-clarify <change>` route (+ delete-`plan.md` note); stop template, gate-fail, and task-fail guidance name the change; `{driver}` claim scoped (fail/recovery commands deliberately driver-less); decision heuristics made **disjoint** (code-fails-correct-requirement → Fix code; requirement-itself-wrong → Revise requirements). fab-proceed: `/git-branch` dropped from the `/fab-new`-prefixed dispatch rows (Step 11 creates the branch inline) and the promptless fab-new dispatch defined as defer-and-surface (see [planning-skills.md](/pipeline/planning-skills.md)). New design decisions "Ship-Time Skills Take an Explicit Change…" and "Recovery Guidance Is Override-Aware". 13 SPEC mirrors + `docs/specs/skills.md`/`glossary.md`/`architecture.md` § Git Integration updated same-PR. (w7dp)

- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **SRAD scoring coherence** (skills-audit batch 4/5, Theme 6). `_srad.md`: grade bands are now **half-open thresholds** — composite ≥ 85 Certain, ≥ 60 Confident, ≥ 30 Tentative, else Unresolved (closed integer bands left continuous composites like 59.85/84.5 ungraded); the **Critical Rule has one numeric definition** — `R < 25 AND A < 25` (§ Critical Rule prose and Worked Example 1 now cite `< 25` explicitly; the 0–39 "Low" band is descriptive only); Worked Example 3 re-dimensioned (S:50 R:95 A:100 D:100 → 86) so its Certain grade is arithmetically reachable and its tail corrected (Certain decisions ARE recorded in the Assumptions summary); Example 2 gained explicit scores (composite 77); the autonomy table gained a **covering note** for the two remaining declaring skills (fab-draft = fab-new's column exactly; fab-clarify = the escape valve itself); **omit-when-zero is display-only** — generated artifacts (intake.md/plan.md) ALWAYS carry `## Assumptions`, `0 assumptions.` footer when empty, keeping `fab score` parsing uniform. `_generation.md`: the Plan Generation walk gained an explicit numbered **`## Assumptions` emission step** (step 7, before the renumbered write step — three grades, Scores required, footer), and Intake step 4 notes the always-present rule. `fab-new.md`: Output template reordered — the Assumptions block is the final content block immediately before `Next:` per `_srad`'s SHALL. ff/fff cycle-cap prose updated to the `{max_cycles}` knob (default 3 — see [execution-skills.md](/pipeline/execution-skills.md)). `docs/specs/srad.md` aligned (Scores required on every row, all four grades recorded at intake, half-open bands, `<25` Critical-Rule phrasing, example composites corrected to 15.75/42.75/94.35). SPEC mirrors updated: SPEC-_srad, SPEC-_generation, SPEC-fab-new (+ SPEC-fab-clarify — see [clarify.md](/pipeline/clarify.md)). (c5tr)
- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **`Next:` lines rule-derived** (skills-audit batch 5/5, root-cause guard): `fab-new.md` and `fab-draft.md` closing `Next:` lines now derive at runtime per `_preamble.md` § Lookup Procedure (intake-state row, default first — including `/fab-proceed`, which the hardcoded enumerations had drifted to omit); behavior unchanged, the derivation rule replaces the literal list (`fab-setup.md` already derived — verified in place). **`_generation` consumer groups corrected**: the partial's header/intro now names `/fab-continue` in **both** consumer groups (Intake Generation via its intake-`active` regeneration row + Plan Generation at apply entry) and drops the stale "auto-clarify" orchestration residue (removed in j6cs). (d9rs)
- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **fab-new Step 11 branch table hardened** (twin of `git-branch.md` Step 4, behavior-flagged): the first-match-wins table grew from 5 to **6 rows** — new row 3 checks out **remote-only** branches with `git checkout --track "origin/{name}"` instead of recreating a divergent local; the rename guard (row 5) now also renames when the current local-only branch resolves to the **same** change being branched (worktree-placeholder scenario — no more stray placeholder branch); a non-blocking **dirty-tree note** (` — note: {dirty_count} uncommitted change(s) carried over from {old_branch}`) is appended on `checkout -b`/`branch -m` rows (warn, never stash-prompt). fab-new-specific divergence: `{dirty_count}` excludes `fab/changes/{name}/` so the change's own just-created artifacts don't fire the note on every run (git-branch counts the full porcelain). Context commands extended (porcelain count, `origin/{name}` verify); keep-in-sync comments updated in both twins. git-pr's "already shipped" no-op re-run is now conditioned on an OPEN PR. `SPEC-fab-new.md` mirror updated same-PR. (g8st)
- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **Twins single-sourced** (skills-review batch 4/4, f031/f007/f071/f032). `fab-draft.md` rewritten as a **thin delta over fab-new** (169 → 48 lines): read the deployed `fab-new` SKILL.md, execute its Pre-flight/Arguments/Steps 0–9 with deltas — Step 9 tail = NOT activated (user must `/fab-switch`), prominent SKIP of Steps 10–11 (no activation, no git), Output minus Activated/Branch lines + Activation Preamble `Next:`, activation/git error rows dropped; the byte-identical ~120-line Steps 0–9 copy deleted (deliberately NOT moved into `_generation.md` — fab-continue/ff/fff also load it). `fab-ff.md` (138 → 64) and `fab-fff.md` (166 → 105) rewritten as **thin wrappers over the new `_pipeline.md` bracket** (`helpers: [_generation, _review, _srad, _pipeline]`; parameter tables set `{driver}`/`{terminal}`; fff keeps its Steps 4–5 ship/review-pr + driver error rows; ff adds none) — resolving the "Two gates" vs single-intake-gate drift and restoring the post-bail `/fab-clarify` guidance to fff via the shared stop text. `fab-new.md` Step 11 compressed from 5 prose cases (~56 lines) to one **first-match-wins condition/command/report table** (~15 lines) + context commands + a keep-in-sync comment referencing `git-branch.md` Step 4 — report strings byte-identical, rename guard preserved, still inline (no runtime `/git-branch` delegation). Updated the "/fab-ff and /fab-fff Keep Behavioral Descriptions" design decision. SPEC mirrors: SPEC-fab-draft (thin-delta), SPEC-fab-new (Step 11 table), SPEC-fab-ff/SPEC-fab-fff (wrapper shape), new SPEC-_pipeline. (szxd)
- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **fab-new backlog-ID collision pre-check exact-ID anchored** (skills-audit batch 2/5, Themes 2+4): Step 3 now runs `fab resolve --id {id}` and compares the canonical 4-char ID for **equality** with `{id}` — a substring hit inside another change's slug (which resolves with a different canonical ID) no longer routes to resume and silently skips creation; the CLI `Change ID already in use` error stays the backlog-only safety net. **fab-proceed's promptless fab-new dispatch defined** (defer-and-surface): the dispatch prompt instructs no questions — would-be-asked SRAD Unresolved decisions are recorded as `Deferred — promptless dispatch` Unresolved rows, returned in the subagent result, and surfaced as informational lines before the `/fab-fff` delegation; the intake gate is the structural backstop (any Unresolved row scores 0.0 per `score.go`). `_srad.md`'s Critical Rule gained the matching **promptless-dispatch carve-out** (cross-referencing fab-proceed; wherever a user is reachable, MUST-ask applies unchanged). fab-proceed's two `/fab-new`-prefixed dispatch rows dropped the stale `/git-branch` chain (no-op since #322 — fab-new Step 11 creates the branch inline); `/fab-switch`-prefixed rows keep it. The ff/fff post-bail stop guidance is now the executable, override-aware `/fab-continue <change> intake` → `/fab-clarify <change>` route (see [execution-skills.md](/pipeline/execution-skills.md)). New § Promptless Dispatch subsection; "SRAD Autonomy Framework" and "ID-Collision Re-Runs Route to Resume" design decisions updated. SPEC mirrors (SPEC-fab-new, SPEC-fab-proceed, SPEC-_srad, SPEC-fab-ff, SPEC-fab-fff) updated same-PR. (w7dp)
- **Update** [preflight](/pipeline/preflight.md) — refactor: CLI Single-Sourcing & Doc Conformance (#402)
- **Update** [preflight](/pipeline/preflight.md) — feat: Pane Map Display State (#394)
- **Update** [preflight](/pipeline/preflight.md) — fix: CLI Exit-Code Contract Conformance (#395)
- **Update** [preflight](/pipeline/preflight.md) — Corrected the `display_stage`/`display_state` field docs to the actual derivation: `display_state` draws from the full six-value set (`active`, `ready`, `done`, `failed`, `pending`, `skipped`) — the prior "(`active`, `done`, or `pending`)" was stale — and `display_stage` is the five-tier walk now including the `failed` tier added in dkn3 (first active, else first failed, else first ready, else last done/skipped, else first pending). Preflight output for a review-failed change with nothing active is now `display_stage: review` / `display_state: failed` instead of `apply`/`done`. No preflight-side code change — the value flows from `status.DisplayStage` (see [change-lifecycle.md](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md)). (dkn3)
- **Update** [preflight](/pipeline/preflight.md) — Doc reconciliation only: the routing derivation's all-done fallback is `review-pr` (matches `CurrentStage` in status.go), not `hydrate` — same drift corrected in [schemas.md](/pipeline/schemas.md) and [change-lifecycle.md](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md). No preflight behavior change. (k4ge)
- **Update** [preflight](/pipeline/preflight.md) — Wording-only sync with the config-parser consolidation (binary-review B4, F26): `checkSyncStaleness` now reads `fab_version` through the shared `internal/config` loader (`config.Load(fabRoot)` + nil-safe `GetFabVersion()`) instead of a local anonymous-struct parse. Silent-skip semantics unchanged — any read/parse failure or empty value still means no warning, advisory only. Also (F28): the Skill Integration section's command-telemetry references updated from the long-deleted `logman.sh command` to `fab log command` (best-effort — always exits 0 given valid usage, no shell guard needed). (ye8r)

- **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — **`workflow.yaml` retired — schema authority is the Go state machine** (skills-audit batch 4/5, Theme 5). Deleted `src/kit/schemas/workflow.yaml` (and with it the `src/kit/schemas/` directory): zero consumers, and the file had drifted a full pipeline generation — it still defined the pre-1.10.0 7-stage pipeline with `spec` while `docs/specs/user-flow.md:201` called it the source of truth. That line now points at `src/go/fab/internal/status` (transitions + side-effects) and `src/go/fab/internal/statusfile` (stage order + progress schema). Retired, not regenerated — a fresh declarative artifact would re-create the unenforced drift surface; code + tests are the schema. This doc's Overview / definition / query / design-principles sections rewritten accordingly (stale `statusman.sh` references swept to the `fab status`/`fab preflight` CLI surface). Future-enhancement "Stage hooks" marked shipped — the live `stage_hooks` config behavior is documented in `_cli-fab.md` § stage_hooks (see [change-lifecycle.md](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md)). (c5tr)
- **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — `fab score` truth-telling (binary-review batch B2/6). **Normal-mode failure surfacing**: `score.Compute` now returns — and the CLI exits non-zero with stderr detail, per the k4ge RunE routing — `.status.yaml` load failures (previously: write-back block silently skipped, `change_type` defaulted to `feat`), confidence write-back failures (`SetConfidence`/`SetConfidenceFuzzy`, previously `_ =`-discarded), `.history.jsonl` confidence-log append failures, and `intake.md` read failures; YAML on stdout only on full success. **Gate integrity**: `countGrades(content)` parses caller-read content via the new `internal/lines` helper's `Split` (composing with mz4q's F02 content-based signature and F06 classified `os.ReadFile` errors in `CheckGate`/`Compute`) — read failure distinguishable from a missing Assumptions table, truncation-driven fail→pass gate flips impossible. Hook caller's `if err == nil` guard untouched (hook path stays best-effort). New "Normal-mode failure surfacing" paragraph extending the k4ge `--check-gate` exit-contract section. (hv7t)
- **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — CLI exit-code contract conformance (skills-audit batch 1/5). **Transition target-state validation**: `lookupTransition` now validates the resolved target against the stage's allowed states via a `validateTarget` helper (both override and default paths) — `advance ship`, `advance review-pr`, and `skip intake` are rejected non-zero with no write, instead of writing a forbidden state that permanently bricks `fab preflight`; the dead `stageTransitions["review-pr"]["advance"]` override row is a recorded deletion candidate. **`fab score --check-gate` exit contract**: exits non-zero on `gate: fail` (YAML intact on stdout, `intake gate failed: score {x} below threshold {y}` on stderr) — the single intake gate is now observable by `/fab-ff`/`/fab-fff`; exit 0 on pass. **Iterations-preserving cascade**: the `reset`/`skip` cascade keeps `stage_metrics` entries with `iterations > 0` (only timing fields cleared; zero-iteration entries still deleted), so review cycle counts survive the fail+reset rework choreography and `fab pr-meta` reports real cycles. Also corrected pre-existing drift in § Progression: the all-done routing fallback is `review-pr` (`CurrentStage`, status.go), not `hydrate`. (k4ge)
- **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — **Per-stage allowed-states + transition-event tables enumerated** (binary-review B6, F39): the Stages and Transitions bullets now carry the full `AllowedStates` table and the complete event table (from-states, targets, stage applicability — incl. the review/review-pr `failed→active` start override and the forbidden `advance ship`/`advance review-pr`/`skip intake` combos). Previously this doc described the concepts but never enumerated the tables, so the spec that the new exhaustive matrix test (`internal/status/transitions_test.go`, 216 cells, hand-written expectations) asserts had no documented home. The k4ge iterations-preserving cascade is now regression-tested end-to-end (3-cycle fail→reset→re-finish through public `Fail`/`Reset`/`Finish`, and across the `skip` cascade) — the test passes against current behavior, so the PR #402 "1 cycle" anomaly is NOT a state-machine bug. Also: the retired `workflow.yaml`'s frozen benchmark-fixture copy is gone — `src/benchmark/` implementations and fixtures were deleted in this change (README + RESULTS kept as the decision record). (tb6f)
- **Update** [schemas](/pipeline/schemas.md) — `fab log command` now fully owns the best-effort telemetry contract (binary-review B4, F28): the CLI subcommand always exits 0 given valid usage (cobra arg-count errors exit non-zero before RunE), wrapping `runLogCommand` and printing a one-line `Warning: fab log command: …` to stderr on any internal failure — the paths that previously exited 1 (FabRoot failure, an explicit change arg failing resolve, unwritable `.history.jsonl`) can no longer STOP a pipeline via a forgotten shell guard. The ":telemetry hooks never become new failure modes" posture note in this file is now literally true for `fab log command`. Call-site guard boilerplate (`2>/dev/null || true`) retired from `_preamble.md` (Common-fab-Commands row, key-behaviors bullet, §2 step-4 template, failure rule) and the 5 skill files with explicit calls (`fab-help`, `fab-switch`, `fab-setup`, `fab-operator`, `fab-discuss`), plus `_cli-fab.md`'s fab-log section and the SPEC mirrors. `log review`/`confidence`/`transition` unchanged (fail-loud). `internal/log` untouched — the contract lives at the CLI layer (`cmd/fab/log.go`). (ye8r)

## 2026-06-11
- **Update** [change-lifecycle](/pipeline/change-lifecycle.md) — **git-branch rename guard (f100)**: the local-only-branch case no longer renames unconditionally — `git branch -m` only when the current branch resolves to no other change (`fab change resolve "$(git branch --show-current)"` fails); a branch matching another change diverts to `git checkout -b`, leaving it intact (caveat: the new branch inherits the old change's HEAD). Git Integration table split the no-upstream row into guarded rename vs create-new; the same guard is mirrored in `/fab-new` Step 11 Case 4 (twins kept in sync). **Actionable resolve errors (f124)**: the zero-candidate and missing-`fab/changes/` paths now exit with `No active change. Run /fab-new <description> to start one, or /fab-switch to activate an existing one.` (resolve.go; `_cli-fab.md` rows updated for the changed strings only). **fab-status over-threshold channel (f047)**: documented the Impact line's highlight as ⚠️ prefix + bold (`net > 100` or `excluding.net > 50`, hard-coded) — the prior ANSI-yellow mandate was unsatisfiable (SGR escapes are stripped by the assistant-message render path). **Review-pr timeout (f015/f016)**: documented the Copilot-timeout outcome as non-terminal — stage left `active` for re-run, NOT a clean finish. Also documented under "Pipeline shortcuts" the `/fab-fff` pending message. Go test updates shipped alongside (resolve_test.go; fabhelp_test.go for the six-stage `fab fab-help` pipeline render + 4 new skillToGroupMap entries). (9u91)
- **Update** [clarify](/pipeline/clarify.md) — **fab-clarify becomes the protocol + bulk-confirm home** (skills-review batch 3/4). The `[AUTO-MODE]` Skill Invocation Protocol moved from `_preamble.md` into `fab-clarify.md` § Skill Invocation Protocol (sole referencer; 2-line pointer left in the preamble; the preamble's § Subagent Dispatch mention repointed). Auto Mode retained — user decision: move, not delete; zero behavior change. Bulk-confirm trigger (`confident >= 3` AND `confident > tentative + unresolved`) and semantics are now **single-homed** in `fab-clarify.md` Step 2 — the preamble's verbatim duplicate was cut to a one-sentence pointer. `fab-clarify.md` also gained its first frontmatter `helpers:` key (`[_srad]`) for the extracted SRAD framework. New design decision "`[AUTO-MODE]` Protocol Co-located with Its Sole Referencer"; "Mode Selection" decision updated. `SPEC-fab-clarify.md` mirror and `docs/specs/glossary.md` `[AUTO-MODE]` entry updated. (zc9m)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — Skills-review batch 1 (16 verified findings). **Dispatch validity (f005)**: fab-continue Step 1 intake-ready rows now read "finish intake (auto-activates apply)" (no `start apply` call — `start` only accepts pending/failed and would hard-error); review-fail row uses `reset <change> apply fab-continue`. **Ownership (f006)**: orchestrator owns all `fab status` transitions for `/fab-continue`-behavior subagents — ff/fff dispatch prompts say "do NOT run `fab status` commands; return results only", fab-continue's Apply/Review/Hydrate sections carry the when-subagent skip rule, the hydrate finish moved to the orchestrator (driver = fab-ff/fab-fff), and ship/review-pr stay self-managed by git-pr/git-pr-review (fab-continue re-finishes only if still `active`). **Deterministic pass rule (f012)**: "no must-fix findings (including zero findings) → review passes" — should-fix/nice-to-have never block. **git-pr-review single exit + timeout (f015/f016)**: Steps 1/2/4 route all terminal paths to Step 6 (single exit point, four outcomes {success, failure, no-reviews, timeout}); the Copilot-poll timeout leaves review-pr `active` (no finish, no fail) instead of clean-finishing, Step 6.5 skips the fail/timeout paths, and fab-fff Step 5 reports `Review-PR pending (Copilot review requested, timed out waiting) — re-run /git-pr-review when ready` instead of `Pipeline complete.`. **ff/fff intake-finish (f069/g1-6)**: condition is "if `progress.intake` is not `done`, finish intake" (covers the normal `ready` hand-off). **Review-failed resume guard (g1-5)**: `progress.review == failed` → `fab status start <change> review` first, in all three orchestrators. **Hydrate dedup (g1-3)**: hydrate step 4 gains the merge-without-duplication contract (check for an existing entry referencing the change, update in place); Key Properties idempotency extends to hydrate. Also amended the 8mrz changelog row's stale claim that ANSI matches the fab-status idiom. Go fixes documented elsewhere: fabhelp.go six-stage pipeline + 4 skillToGroupMap entries; resolve.go actionable "No active change." guidance (see change-lifecycle.md). (9u91)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **git-pr-review exit-point exceptions (M.1)**: the 9u91 "single exit point" claim is corrected — Step 6 is the exit for every terminal path after Step 0 **with two named exceptions** that STOP directly: Step 1.5 (invalid `--tool` value, before any review processing) and Step 5 (commit/push failure on the code-fix commit, after `git reset`). The `docs/specs/skills.md` git-pr-review rollup was aligned in the same change (`--tool` accepts `copilot` only, no Codex/Claude cascade, Step-6 routing + the two exceptions). **fab-continue review-pr Fail guard (M.2, behavior-flagged)**: the review-pr dispatch row's Fail branch now carries the same only-if-still-active guard its Pass branch got in 9u91 — git-pr-review's Step 6 runs `fail` itself, so fab-continue runs `fail <change> review-pr` only if the stage is still `active` after the behavior returns (no second CLI-rejected `fail`). (uliv)
- **Update** [execution-skills](/pipeline/execution-skills.md) — **fab-continue stage-conditional helper loading** (skills-review batch 3/4, f122): frontmatter reduced to `helpers: [_srad]`; `_generation` is now read at point of use (apply entry plan-generation path AND the intake-`active` regeneration path) and `_review` at Review Behavior entry — hydrate/ship/review-pr invocations and apply-resumes load neither, saving 8.7–19.2KB per invocation (fab-continue total: 82,514 → 60,788–71,392B depending on stage). `_preamble.md` § Skill Helper Declaration extended to sanction in-body point-of-use reads (stage-conditional loading). fab-ff/fab-fff deliberately keep `[_generation, _review, _srad]` unconditional (f074 refuted — the orchestrator rework loop edits plan.md sections itself). `fab-proceed.md`'s restated Standard Subagent Context 5-file list replaced with a pointer to `_preamble.md` § Standard Subagent Context. New § Stage-Conditional Helper Loading requirements section + "fab-continue Loads Heavyweight Helpers Per Stage" design decision. SPEC-fab-continue/SPEC-fab-ff/SPEC-fab-fff/SPEC-fab-proceed mirrors updated. (zc9m)
- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **Re-run semantics (g1-2)**: added the "Re-Run Semantics (Idempotency)" section — `/fab-new`/`/fab-draft` Step 3 detects an existing non-archived change per ID type (backlog: `fab change resolve {id}`; Linear: issues-array scan `grep -l "{ISSUE_ID}" fab/changes/*/.status.yaml`, single-level glob excludes archive/) and routes to resume (`/fab-switch` + `/fab-continue`) instead of erroring; NL re-run intentionally creates a new change each run; `change.go` error kept as backlog-only safety net. **Idempotency declarations (g1-7)**: fab-new, fab-draft, and git-pr each gained a Key Properties section with an `Idempotent?` row. **Slug drift fixed**: removed the stale claim that a parsed Linear ID prefixes the slug/folder name — slugs SHALL NOT include issue IDs (folder format is `{YYMMDD}-{XXXX}-{slug}`; IDs live in `.status.yaml` `issues` arrays). **Rename-guard twin (f100)**: fab-new Step 11 Case 4 documented as carrying the same rename guard as git-branch Step 4 (rename only when the current branch resolves to no other change; otherwise `git checkout -b` with the inherited-HEAD caveat). **Dispatch wording (f005)**: intake-ready dispatch now reads "finish intake (auto-activates apply)" — no separate `start apply` call. **ff/fff entry guards (f069/g1-6 + g1-5)**: "if `progress.intake` is not `done`, finish intake"; review `failed` → `fab status start <change> review` first. Added the "ID-Collision Re-Runs Route to Resume" design decision. (9u91)
- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **Change-type hook ownership (g3-4, behavior-flagged)**: replaced fab-new/fab-draft Step 6's manual keyword-matching + unconditional `set-change-type` with the hook-owned contract — the PostToolUse intake-write hook infers and writes `change_type` on every intake write (word-boundary regexes incl. `redesign`, first match wins, default `feat`); skills verify via `.status.yaml` (`grep '^change_type:'` — preflight does not emit it) and override via `fab status set-change-type` only if wrong, with the re-fire-overwrites caveat. Removes the skill/hook double-write race; "Change Type Inference" section rewritten as "Change Type (Hook-Owned)". **Collision grep word-anchored (M.4)**: the Linear-ID collision check in both skills is now `grep -lw "{ISSUE_ID}" fab/changes/*/.status.yaml 2>/dev/null` so `DEV-123` no longer matches `DEV-1234`. New design decision "Change-Type Inference Is Hook-Owned". (uliv)
- **Update** [planning-skills](/pipeline/planning-skills.md) — **SRAD framework extracted to the `_srad.md` helper** (skills-review batch 3/4): the full framework (scoring table, weighted-mean aggregation, trapezoidal grades, Critical Rule, autonomy levels, worked examples — Example 1 compressed to one-liner style — artifact markers, Assumptions Summary block) moved verbatim from `_preamble.md` into `src/kit/skills/_srad.md`, declared via frontmatter `helpers:` by exactly the six planning skills (fab-new/fab-draft `[_generation, _srad]`, fab-continue `[_srad]`, fab-ff/fab-fff `[_generation, _review, _srad]`, fab-clarify `[_srad]` — its first helpers key). `_preamble.md` keeps a ~3-line pointer; zero semantic loss. The `[AUTO-MODE]` Skill Invocation Protocol moved from `_preamble.md` into `fab-clarify.md` (sole referencer; Auto Mode retained per user decision). fab-continue's intake-`active` regeneration row now instructs a point-of-use `_generation` read (no longer a frontmatter helper). Updated the "SRAD Autonomy Framework" and "Clarify Mode Selection" design decisions. `SPEC-_srad.md` created; SPEC mirrors for all six planning skills updated. (zc9m)
